jokes.text: Hello, boils and ghouls. I am the crypt-keeper, or should I say master of scare-i-monies?
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'ceremonies' becoming 'scare-i-monies'
jokes.explanation: Standard horror pun, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Priceless, sir. You made the word 'ceremonies' frightening. I know what I did. Hey, can we get going here? Listening to you two is more torture than the torture.
jokes.setup: Character compliments the pun, then another interrupts
jokes.punchline: Claims their banter is worse torture than actual torture happening
jokes.explanation: Meta humor about the show's own forced banter, solid structure

jokes.text: Actually, no, this is worse. Although the right nipple spike seems a little dull.
jokes.setup: Character being tortured considers what's worse
jokes.punchline: Casually critiques the torture equipment quality
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan absurdity, treating torture like product review

jokes.text: Whoa, look at that! My blood's a genius. Fancy Roman numerals and everything.
jokes.punchline: Admires the Roman numerals (XVII) formed by their blood
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag connecting title card to character reaction, very Simpsons-style meta humor

jokes.text: Whoa! That almost tore my head off. Oh, you always find an excuse not to make out.
jokes.punchline: Girl accuses him of using near-death experience as excuse to avoid romance
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of expectations, relationship comedy meets disaster movie

jokes.text: Whoo! A space marshmallow.
jokes.punchline: Homer sees it as food, specifically a marshmallow
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - sees everything through food lens

jokes.text: Dad, no! It could teach us the secret of interstellar travel. If he's so smart, how come he can't stay out of my mouth?
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic: if alien is smart, why is he eating it?
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - perfectly stupid reasoning delivered with confidence

jokes.text: How could you eat that goo? You don't know what galaxy it's from. Marge, I ate it. It's over.
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact dismissal of consequences
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vs Marge dynamic, his casual acceptance of poor decisions

jokes.text: If I can keep down Arby's, I can keep down you.
jokes.setup: Alien blob trying to make Homer sick
jokes.punchline: Homer compares alien to notoriously questionable fast food
jokes.explanation: Great economy and cultural reference, Arby's as benchmark for digestive challenge

jokes.text: Must eat, then poop, then eat some more, then eat while pooping.
jokes.setup: Homer infected by alien blob's hunger
jokes.punchline: Escalating eating schedule reaches absurd multitasking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, though relies on gross-out humor

jokes.text: Still hungry. Dad? Son, let me have a lick at you.
jokes.setup: Homer still consumed by alien hunger
jokes.punchline: Casually asks to taste his own son
jokes.explanation: Dark family comedy, Homer's casual approach to cannibalism

jokes.text: Homer! You won't eat my stuffed peppers, but you'll eat our son? Nag, nag, nag.
jokes.setup: Marge points out the hypocrisy of eating preferences
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses cannibalism concerns as nagging
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - perfect Marge/Homer dynamic with dark twist

jokes.text: Is that the cat? Uh, no, just gas.
jokes.punchline: Homer claims it's gas, but we know he ate the cat
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection, Homer covering up his consumption

jokes.text: No, today's teens have enough problems without me eating them.
jokes.punchline: Suddenly considerate about teenage problems
jokes.explanation: Unexpected compassion from monster Homer, clever social commentary

jokes.text: The flames are sealing in my juices. I'll savor you.
jokes.punchline: Homer talks like a food connoisseur about cooking them
jokes.explanation: Homer applying culinary knowledge to cannibalism, characteristic attention to food

jokes.text: Mmm. Extra-virgin.
jokes.punchline: Comments on their virgin status like olive oil quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food commentary applied to dark situation, excellent economy

jokes.text: Tell my friends I died kissing a girl! No.
jokes.punchline: Girl refuses to lie for him
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff, brutal honesty in final moments

jokes.text: What did we Germans ever do to deserve this? Oh, right.
jokes.setup: German asks rhetorical question about persecution
jokes.punchline: Immediately remembers German history
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: I like big guts and I cannot lie / Double chins with the chafing thighs / When a dude walks in with the hanging jowls / My stomach starts to growl--I'm gettin' hungry
jokes.setup: Sir Mix-a-Lot's 'Baby Got Back' parody setup
jokes.punchline: Converted to Homer's cannibalistic appetite for fat people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody that perfectly fits Homer's character and situation

jokes.text: It's blob rule on the streets of Springfield. And to make matters worse, we're also being attacked by a 50-foot Lenny.
jokes.setup: Reporter describing chaos from blob Homer
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions giant Lenny as additional problem
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and absurdist addition, classic Simpsons randomness

jokes.text: Everyone's paying attention to Homer. I still like you. Thanks, Invisible Carl.
jokes.setup: Lenny feeling ignored despite being giant monster
jokes.punchline: Carl is invisible but still supportive friend
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment maintaining friendship dynamics even in chaos

jokes.text: Hi-de-ho, Bloborino.
jokes.punchline: Adapts greeting to acknowledge Homer's blob form
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders moment - maintains cheerful demeanor even facing monster Homer

jokes.text: Dr. Phil McGraw! You've got a weight problem, and you know it. You're right. Starting tomorrow, no bread before dinner.
jokes.setup: Dr. Phil confronts Homer about his eating problem
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely inadequate diet modification
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's denial and minimal effort at change

jokes.text: Homer, don't sell me an outhouse and tell me it's the Taj Mahal. Yeah, Homer, stop doing that.
jokes.setup: Dr. Phil using metaphor for Homer's BS
jokes.punchline: Someone literally tells Homer to stop the specific behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple literal interpretation of metaphor, mild humor

jokes.text: I have to be honest with you: I can't love a 4,000-ton cannibal. What happened to 'for better or for worse?!'
jokes.punchline: Homer invokes marriage vows for extreme situation
jokes.explanation: Great marriage comedy, Homer using vows to justify cannibalism

jokes.text: Dad, you're eating Dr. Phil. It's amazing. He tastes just like Jeffrey Tambor.
jokes.setup: Someone points out Homer eating Dr. Phil
jokes.punchline: Homer compares taste to Jeffrey Tambor
jokes.explanation: Specific cultural reference for taste comparison, Homer's food obsession

jokes.text: You gotta know when to golem
jokes.setup: Reference to Kenny Rogers song 'The Gambler'
jokes.punchline: Replaces 'hold 'em' with 'golem' for Jewish monster story
jokes.explanation: Simple pun connecting song lyric to story theme

jokes.text: Finally, I'd like to announce that starting Monday, this show will be broadcast in HDTV. Here's how I'll look. Heh? That's right: look at your hero!
jokes.punchline: HD reveals his grotesque appearance in detail
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about HD revealing unwanted details, typical Krusty self-deprecation

jokes.text: Aren't you coming, Bart? Nah, I'm gonna go backstage and complain. This Krusty Brand alarm clock sprays acid in your face.
jokes.punchline: Reveals dangerously defective Krusty product
jokes.explanation: Good character moment and absurd product danger, typical Krusty merchandise

jokes.text: Cool! It's that clown car all those midgets drowned in.
jokes.punchline: Excited about tragic prop history
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Bart's morbid fascination, good character consistency

jokes.text: Legendary defender of the Jewish people, like Alan Dershowitz, but with a conscience.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded insult to Alan Dershowitz
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural reference with specific insult, good Krusty voice

jokes.text: I kid Alan. We're friends.
jokes.punchline: Quickly covers with fake friendship claim
jokes.explanation: Quick backtrack typical of celebrity damage control

jokes.text: So the wife says, 'I don't have to pay for it. I put it on my charge plate.' You suck, clown! You made my lady cry!
jokes.punchline: Audience member angry about making his girlfriend cry
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between dated comedy and modern reaction

jokes.text: I'm gonna need a shoebox full of blow to get through this dreck.
jokes.punchline: Needs drugs to perform his own show
jokes.explanation: Dark Krusty self-awareness about his declining career

jokes.text: Bart, there's something I have to tell you father to son. I passed out on your turtle and killed it. Someday you'll thank me.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous accident and inexplicable claim Bart will be grateful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic and parenting, absurd conclusion about gratitude

jokes.text: Can't you read my writing? I didn't say kick Homer's 'walls'.
jokes.punchline: Kicked Homer's 'balls' not 'walls'
jokes.explanation: Simple visual pun about handwriting/violence, mildly juvenile

jokes.text: Yo, Simpson. Give us your lunch money. Mmm... I don't think so. In fact, why don't you give me your lunch money?
jokes.punchline: Bart confidently reverses the demand
jokes.explanation: Good power reversal, Bart's new confidence with golem backup

jokes.text: Finally, someone who does whatever I say. Hey, Bart, I shaved my head like you told me. Get lost. Yes, Master.
jokes.setup: Bart enjoys having control over golem
jokes.punchline: Milhouse also follows Bart's commands but gets dismissed
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast, Milhouse's pathetic devotion vs golem's power

jokes.text: Did your mystical Jewish monster beat up those bullies? Oh, it's always the Jews' fault.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions Bart about the golem
jokes.punchline: Bart deflects with antisemitic stereotype comment
jokes.explanation: Bart using historical scapegoating to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: Finally, I can talk! This is the voice I've got? Sounds like I should be selling egg creams in Brighton Beach.
jokes.punchline: Disappointed with his stereotypical Jewish accent
jokes.explanation: Self-aware stereotype humor, golem commenting on his own voice

jokes.text: That's what we call Jewish humor. You don't have to understand it 'cause the words sound funny. Meshuggeneh. Hilarious.
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to funny-sounding Yiddish words
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on ethnic humor tropes, Krusty's cynical view

jokes.text: Of course I feel bad. My stomach feels like it's hosting the Chabad telethon and Norm Crosby's going long.
jokes.setup: Golem asked if he feels bad about violence
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Jewish cultural reference for stomach discomfort
jokes.explanation: Specific cultural reference with good structure, character-appropriate guilt

jokes.text: 'Kill the czar'? Now that's an old one.
jokes.punchline: Comments on the historical age of the command
jokes.explanation: Good historical humor, golem as witness to long history

jokes.text: Skinner! I wish we'd been closer. Get him out of here, Willie. You'll make better mulch than you did a man.
jokes.punchline: Willie's practical and insulting disposal plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie character voice, practical Scottish brutality

jokes.text: I may be bifurcated, but I still have feelings.
jokes.setup: Skinner cut in half but still alive
jokes.punchline: Uses academic vocabulary to describe being cut in half
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character - using pretentious vocabulary even while dying horribly

jokes.text: I feel so guilty. I've mangled and maimed 37 people and I told a telemarketer I was busy when I wasn't. I'm not a good man.
jokes.punchline: Equates mass violence with lying to telemarketer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of mass murder with minor social lie, perfect guilt hierarchy

jokes.text: He sure is neurotic for a monster. The cure to any man's problems is a good relationship. Yeah, get him a woman. That'll end his misery.
jokes.setup: Discussing golem's neuroses and suggesting relationship
jokes.punchline: Cynical view that relationships cause misery
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship cynicism, predictable punchline

jokes.text: The surprise would be if I found some good live theater in this town. Then people could have some drinks, watch Fiddler. Very nice.
jokes.punchline: Wants sophisticated Jewish cultural entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency, golem's cultural sophistication

jokes.text: Ugh! What's with this outfit? It looks like a lion ate a parrot and then threw up.
jokes.punchline: Vivid disgusting metaphor for colorful dress
jokes.explanation: Vivid visual metaphor with good structure, classic Selma complaints

jokes.text: Not so fast. You're wanted for the murder of Seymour Skinner. Oh, come on, Chief. We've got a lat kebar downstairs. Latkes? What the hell are those? They're pan-fried... Case dismissed.
jokes.punchline: Drops charges for Jewish food he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum incompetence, distracted by food

jokes.text: I don't get it. What's so 'Great' about this Depression? Well, I like how everything's sepia-toned. Makes me feel all nostalgic.
jokes.setup: Character confused about Great Depression name
jokes.punchline: Other character likes the visual filter effect
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about TV/film visual conventions for historical periods

jokes.text: I never thought it would come to this when I fought in the first World War. First World War? Why you keep callin' it that? Oh, you'll see.
jokes.setup: Old man calls it 'first' World War
jokes.punchline: Ominous prediction of World War II
jokes.explanation: Good historical irony, ominous foreshadowing of WWII

jokes.text: First World War? Why you keep callin' it that? / Oh, you'll see.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by 'First' World War terminology
jokes.punchline: Ominous hint about second war coming
jokes.explanation: Good historical irony, works well for period setting

jokes.text: So, it's a war of the worlds. Good thing we got the sun on our side.
jokes.punchline: Thinks the sun will help them fight Mars
jokes.explanation: Misunderstanding of astronomy/warfare, simple confusion humor

jokes.text: Okay, boys, we're under attack. Let's drive 'em out of town the way we did with the Irish. Hey, I'm Irish. Oh, wait, I'm Polish.
jokes.punchline: Officer confused about his own ethnicity
jokes.explanation: Historical ethnic tensions humor, character confusion about identity

jokes.text: Now they're riding horses in the rain. Now they're playing the xylophone while bowling near an airport.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly random and mundane activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation from threatening to bizarrely specific mundane activities

jokes.text: Well, I just hope our saucy little radio prank will give these 'folks,' as you call them, a giggle, a gasp, and provide a good lead-in for Misters Amos and Andy.
jokes.setup: Welles explaining his radio play intentions
jokes.punchline: Condescending attitude and reference to racist radio show
jokes.explanation: Historical characterization and reference to problematic entertainment of era

jokes.text: Wait, did you get my sweater out of the front seat? Uh, yeah. Well, where is it? I'll go get it.
jokes.setup: Someone claims they got sweater from car
jokes.punchline: Admits they didn't actually get it
jokes.explanation: Simple lie caught, mild relationship humor

jokes.text: Wait, I have an idea. The Martians are only killing humans, so we should pretend to be animals. Ingenious. Everyone, remove your clothes and wallow in filth.
jokes.setup: Plan to pretend to be animals
jokes.punchline: Immediate enthusiastic adoption of degrading behavior
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from plan to immediate extreme implementation

jokes.text: Martians? You do know that radio broadcast was a hoax? Yeah, it was an Orson Welles radio play of War of the Worlds.
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals it was just a radio show
jokes.explanation: Standard Lisa as voice of reason, historical reference resolution

jokes.text: Foolish Earthlings. Now is the perfect time to strike. They'll think it's another hoax.
jokes.setup: Humans just learned radio show was fake
jokes.punchline: Real aliens use this as cover for actual invasion
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic twist, perfect villain logic using human gullibility

jokes.text: How's it feel when the hoax is on the other foot? I must admit it's unpleasant.
jokes.punchline: Welles admits the reversal is uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Mixed metaphor pun, mild turnabout humor

jokes.text: You fool. You've doomed us all. Eh, tell it to the Great Gildersleeve.
jokes.punchline: Chief dismisses with old radio show reference
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate reference, Chief's dismissive attitude

jokes.text: You said we'd be greeted as liberators. Don't worry. We still have the people's hearts and minds. I don't know. I'm starting to think 'Operation: Enduring Occupation' was a bad idea.
jokes.punchline: Clear references to Iraq War rhetoric and problems
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire using exact Iraq War phrases, very topical for 2006

jokes.text: We had to invade. They were working on weapons of mass disintegration. Sure they were.
jokes.setup: Kang justifies invasion with WMD claim
jokes.punchline: Kodos's sarcastic response showing they knew it was false
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire about WMD justifications for war, very pointed
